summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #228: add the framework-agnostic FileTrigger core in ars-components as a stateless, DOM-free attribute mapper with a typed picker-opening intent API instead of direct DOM activation.
summary.outcome: FileTrigger agnostic core fully implemented with TDD, 100% llvm-cov coverage, 27 mutation mutants (20 caught, 7 unviable, 0 missed), all verification gates passed including cargo xci-fast, and a non-draft PR opened with Closes #228.

repo: GitHub Project board status must be moved to 'In Progress' before any code work begins — enforced by repo workflow, not just convention.
cargo xci-fast runs 10 sequential steps (fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, spec snippets, variant coverage, mutual exclusion, snapshot count) and must pass before any commit/push.
cargo mutants is a mandatory agnostic-component gate; missed mutants must be triaged and either tested or skip-listed with justification in .cargo/mutants.toml.
The post-implementation audit skill (.agents/skills/post-implementation-audit/SKILL.md) is required and must run at least two 'anything else missing?' rounds before the diff is presentable.
utility/download_trigger is the canonical pattern model for new stateless attribute-mapper components: no Machine, inline tests, alloc/core/ars_core/ars_i18n only.

Spec files must be updated in three places when changing a core API: spec/components/, spec/leptos-components/, and spec/dioxus-components/ — plus spec/foundation/02-component-catalog.md for prop summaries.
cargo xtask spec validate covers 340 spec files and must pass; spec/AGENTS.md governs spec authoring rules.
PR commits require co-author trailer and must be non-draft with 'Closes #NNN'; snapshot-reviewed label applied when insta snapshots change.

code.finding: Canonical implementation pattern for stateless API components: Props struct with builder methods, Api::new(props, locale, messages), root_attrs/trigger_attrs/input_attrs returning AttrMap, part_attrs dispatch via ConnectApi, inline #[cfg(test)] module with snapshot assertions.

OpenPickerIntent is a typed marker struct (data-light), not an ID or DOM target. Returns Some when enabled, None when disabled. Adapters own the concrete NodeRef or platform picker handle and resolve the intent themselves.
Spec-conformance tests assert Part::scope(), Part::all() order (root/trigger/input), and part name strings — these are the three required anatomy assertions for every new input component.

Original spec incorrectly implied agnostic open_file_picker() with DOM .click() activation. Required correction to open_picker_intent() -> Option<OpenPickerIntent> with explicit language that adapters resolve via their own input ref or platform handle.

workflow: cargo mutants baseline breaks when unrelated tests fail — use '-- <filter>' to scope baseline to the module under test (e.g., '-- file_trigger') to isolate mutation signal from pre-existing failures.

cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject must not use a positional test filter; pass filter after '--' separator. The full-package run will fail on pre-existing snapshot failures unrelated to the new module.

TDD red-green cycle: apply_patch with tests first, run cargo test to confirm compile failure (red), then patch implementation (green). Snapshot assertions are a separate acceptance step via cargo insta accept.
llvm-cov summary-only JSON output via --json --summary-only --output-path is the efficient way to get coverage metrics without full annotated output.
Nightly rustfmt may produce different formatting than stable; always run cargo +nightly fmt --all after implementation and after adding tests, not just once at the end.

friction: cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject is blocked by pre-existing data-display snapshot failures (meter/progress/stat % label assertions) unrelated to FileTrigger — full package snapshot gate cannot pass without fixing those first.
cargo mutants baseline failed on the same pre-existing data-display test failures, requiring a workaround filter '-- file_trigger' to isolate the new module's mutation surface.
The initial dependency build for cargo test took unusually long (many write_stdin polling cycles) before reaching the actual test binary execution.
cargo insta test positional filter syntax was rejected; required '-- file_trigger' separator form rather than passing filter directly after the package flag.
Nightly fmt produced formatting drift twice during the session — once after initial implementation and once after adding mutation-gap tests — requiring two separate fmt fix+check cycles.
open_items: Pre-existing data-display snapshot failures (meter/progress/stat % label assertions) block the full cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject gate — needs a separate fix before that gate can be used as a package-wide health check.

Leptos adapter component implementation (crates/ars-leptos) is explicitly out of scope for this PR — a follow-up issue/task is needed to wire up the FileTrigger picker intent via NodeRef in the Leptos adapter.
Dioxus adapter component implementation (crates/ars-dioxus) similarly deferred — adapter must resolve OpenPickerIntent using its own platform file picker abstraction.

cargo llvm-cov coverage was verified via summary-only JSON (100% functions/lines/regions) but no branch-level annotation review was done — consider a detailed annotated pass if branch coverage gaps are a concern.
